jokes.text: Oh, yeah, I wish I got a model for Christmas. Mm!
jokes.setup: Nick mentions getting a model airplane for Christmas
jokes.punchline: Will pretends to misunderstand 'model' as a fashion model
jokes.explanation: Classic Will Smith innuendo with good delivery timing, fits his flirtatious character but relies on predictable double entendre

jokes.text: Five-nine, 120. Bloo-bloo-blap-blap! Boom!
jokes.setup: Will continues the model airplane misunderstanding
jokes.punchline: Will makes exaggerated sound effects describing his imaginary model's measurements
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy sounds and Will's signature style, but the joke is becoming labored

jokes.text: Wouldn't that be hard to put together?
jokes.setup: Will's sexual innuendo about models
jokes.punchline: Nick innocently continues talking about model airplanes, creating double meaning
jokes.explanation: Excellent innocent-meets-innuendo comedy. Nick's childlike delivery makes the accidental double entendre funnier

jokes.text: I'm the greatest and I'm pretty. You know I made you, Howard.
jokes.setup: Will imitating Muhammad Ali on TV
jokes.punchline: Classic Ali catchphrase with personal address to Howard Cosell
jokes.explanation: Good impersonation humor, historically accurate Ali reference that fits the boxing theme

jokes.text: Now I'm gonna get medieval on you.
jokes.setup: Phil's lecture about sportsmanship in boxing
jokes.punchline: Little Nick immediately threatens medieval violence
jokes.explanation: Great contrast between Phil's civilized boxing lecture and Nick's immediate violent threat. The line is pop culture reference but well-placed

jokes.text: We ain't really get to the sportsmanship part yet.
jokes.setup: Nick's medieval violence threat
jokes.punchline: Will's casual acknowledgment that they skipped the lesson
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan follow-up that acknowledges the irony without overselling it

jokes.text: I'm not going because I don't want to. And you can't make me.
jokes.setup: Phil giving excuses about work to avoid the gym
jokes.punchline: Phil breaks down into childish honesty and defiance
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment - dignified judge Phil reverting to childish tantrum, perfectly delivered escalation

jokes.text: Oh, you're right, fluorescent lights. I should put on more blush.
jokes.setup: Will tells Hilary they're going to the gym
jokes.punchline: Hilary completely misses the point and focuses on makeup lighting
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary moment - completely self-absorbed and missing obvious context for pure vanity concerns

jokes.text: You're an idiot.
jokes.setup: Carlton singing 'Maniac' while exercising
jokes.punchline: Ashley's blunt assessment of her brother
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective sibling insult, Ashley's deadpan delivery makes it work

jokes.text: I think you look like a family-size tube of Aquafresh.
jokes.setup: Carlton asking how he looks in his workout outfit
jokes.punchline: Will compares him to toothpaste packaging
jokes.explanation: Brilliant visual comparison that's both specific and universally recognizable. The family-size detail makes it even funnier

jokes.text: I stand corrected.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey calling Carlton's outfit the most ridiculous costume ever, then seeing Ashley's work uniform
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's dry acknowledgment that Ashley's uniform is even worse
jokes.explanation: Perfect Geoffrey moment - his formal politeness makes the insult funnier, excellent timing

jokes.text: Gee, wouldn't that suck?
jokes.setup: Ashley complaining about wearing the same uniform every day
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's sarcastic response while wearing his identical butler uniform daily
jokes.explanation: Excellent ironic humor - Geoffrey's personal situation makes the sarcasm perfect, and Ashley's obliviousness adds to it

jokes.text: I'm gonna float like a butterfly and stink like a bee.
jokes.setup: Nick preparing for boxing lessons
jokes.punchline: Nick mixes up Muhammad Ali's famous 'sting like a bee' with 'stink'
jokes.explanation: Classic kid malapropism of famous quote, fits Nick's character and the boxing theme well

jokes.text: Tight as them pants are, you probably started a forest fire in your drawers.
jokes.setup: Carlton saying he's feeling the burn from exercise
jokes.punchline: Will suggests Carlton's tight pants are causing friction burns
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy observation with vivid imagery, classic Will roasting Carlton's fashion choices

jokes.text: I always scream like a girl when I'm working out.
jokes.setup: Carlton gets trapped under gym equipment and screams for help
jokes.punchline: Carlton's sarcastic excuse for his embarrassing moment
jokes.explanation: Decent recovery line from Carlton trying to save face, but relies on dated gender stereotypes

jokes.text: Well, according to this, you're a can of Crisco.
jokes.setup: Carlton claiming he has a good base fitness level
jokes.punchline: Trainer's blunt assessment comparing Carlton to cooking shortening
jokes.explanation: Sharp, visual insult that's both specific and cutting, good setup/payoff structure

jokes.text: But spread over a good base, right?
jokes.setup: Being compared to Crisco
jokes.punchline: Carlton desperately trying to find the positive in the insult
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Carlton's desperate optimism, extends the Crisco metaphor cleverly

jokes.text: You gotta work them yourself, Hil.
jokes.setup: Hilary asking how to turn on exercise equipment
jokes.punchline: Will's explanation that reveals Hilary's complete disconnect from physical activity
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Hilary's cluelessness about exercise, though somewhat predictable

jokes.text: Ew! I'm starting to sweat.
jokes.setup: Hilary beginning to exercise
jokes.punchline: Hilary's disgusted reaction to normal bodily function
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary character moment - her vanity making normal exercise revolting to her

jokes.text: That was a heck of a workout. What do you say we hit the showers?
jokes.setup: Carlton counting to 100 (presumably sit-ups or push-ups)
jokes.punchline: Carlton acts like he did intense exercise when he likely did very little
jokes.explanation: Carlton's delusion about his fitness level, the enthusiasm contrasts with likely minimal actual exercise

jokes.text: What you say I bring the oils?
jokes.setup: Stan suggesting massage after workout
jokes.punchline: Stan's creepy offer to bring massage oils
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable trainer moment, predictable creepy gym trainer trope but gets reaction

jokes.text: Oh. Oh, no, no. Hell, no.
jokes.setup: Stan's inappropriate massage oil suggestion
jokes.punchline: Carlton's increasingly emphatic rejection
jokes.explanation: Good escalating rejection, Carlton's proper character makes his emphatic 'hell no' funnier

jokes.text: Dog must be dipped at a 60-degree angle and rotated until completely coated.
jokes.setup: Ashley learning to dip corn dogs at work
jokes.punchline: Overly technical instructions for simple fast food task
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist workplace humor - overcomplicating simple tasks with pseudo-scientific precision

jokes.text: Churn, baby, churn
jokes.setup: Teaching Ashley to make lemonade
jokes.punchline: Corporate-mandated song about churning lemonade
jokes.explanation: Parody of 'Burn, Baby, Burn' applied to menial labor. Absurd but not brilliant

jokes.text: Ashley, singing is what a Dippity Dog do.
jokes.setup: Ashley refusing to sing while working
jokes.punchline: Manager's earnest explanation of corporate singing policy
jokes.explanation: Absurd corporate culture satire, the grammar construction makes it funnier

jokes.text: You know, I got a better idea. Why don't you get back to your workout, you no-good, yellow-bellied waste of skin!
jokes.setup: Stan offering Carlton a protein shake
jokes.punchline: Stan's sudden transformation into aggressive drill sergeant
jokes.explanation: Good character flip from friendly to militaristic, the old-timey insults add flavor

jokes.text: I don't believe the gold package said anything about rudeness.
jokes.setup: Stan screaming insults at Carlton
jokes.punchline: Carlton's polite consumer complaint about abuse
jokes.explanation: Excellent Carlton moment - treating military-style abuse as a customer service issue, perfect character integration

jokes.text: Oh, no, no. You know, cute little gloves, nice tight little outfit. Works for you, cutie.
jokes.setup: Will discovers Helena is the boxing instructor
jokes.punchline: Will's patronizing, flirtatious response to female boxer
jokes.explanation: Sets up Will's sexist assumptions about female boxers, creates tension for comeuppance

jokes.text: Ain't nobody ever heard of Muhammad Ali Sheedy, you know. You know, it's Sonny Liston, it's not Sonny and Cher Liston.
jokes.setup: Will defending his view that female boxers aren't common
jokes.punchline: Will makes up female versions of famous boxers with female celebrity names
jokes.explanation: Creative wordplay mixing boxing legends with female celebrities, though setup is a bit wordy

jokes.text: Oh, no... You know what? My mom told me never hit a lady.
jokes.setup: Helena challenging Will to spar
jokes.punchline: Will's excuse revealing his fear behind fake chivalry
jokes.explanation: Classic excuse setup for Will's fear, the mama reference fits his character

jokes.text: Because you're not fast enough?
jokes.setup: Will's chivalrous excuse
jokes.punchline: Helena cuts through the BS to the real reason
jokes.explanation: Sharp comeback that cuts through Will's excuse to his insecurity, good economy and bite

jokes.text: I can't. I can't.
jokes.setup: Will in boxing stance ready to fight Helena
jokes.punchline: Will's inability to hit her despite his earlier bravado
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to Will's machismo - when it comes to actual action, he can't follow through

jokes.text: Your mama. Mama said knock you out.
jokes.setup: Helena taunting Will to hit her
jokes.punchline: Will finally motivated by 'your mama' insult, quotes LL Cool J
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - mama jokes are Will's trigger, pop culture reference fits the era and character

jokes.text: Wow, I never seen a freak up close before.
jokes.setup: Kids looking at Will after he was beaten by Helena
jokes.punchline: Kid's cruel observation about Will's defeat
jokes.explanation: Mean-spirited kid humor, adds to Will's humiliation but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Stop right there or I'll get my sister to come after you!
jokes.setup: Will trying to scare off the mocking kids
jokes.punchline: Will's threat reveals he needs his sister's protection
jokes.explanation: Good emasculation joke - Will's tough guy image completely deflated, needs family protection

jokes.text: You bring your sister over here, I'll knock her block off!
jokes.setup: Will threatening to get his sister
jokes.punchline: Kid's dismissive response shows no fear even of Will's sister
jokes.explanation: Escalates Will's humiliation - even his threats of backup are dismissed by children

jokes.text: How should I know? You expect me to keep in touch with a wuss like that?
jokes.setup: Geoffrey telling supportive story about a friend who overcame similar defeat
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey reveals he abandoned the friend for being weak
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - sets up supportive story then reveals Geoffrey's brutal honesty and shallow loyalty

jokes.text: Could be worse. Could be laughingstock of gym like you.
jokes.setup: Will asking how Carlton is feeling after trainer abuse
jokes.punchline: Carlton points out Will's situation is worse than his own
jokes.explanation: Good brother dynamic - Carlton finding silver lining in Will's greater humiliation

jokes.text: The sheer humiliation alone...
jokes.setup: Phil trying to comfort Will about losing to a woman
jokes.punchline: Phil keeps adding to Will's humiliation while trying to help
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of well-meaning but hurtful comfort, Phil's verbose nature makes it worse

jokes.text: Well, at least I didn't get beat up by a girl.
jokes.setup: Ashley complaining about her bad day at work
jokes.punchline: Ashley's casual dismissal of Will's day while wearing her ridiculous uniform
jokes.explanation: Perfect sibling burn - Ashley's terrible day doesn't compare to Will's humiliation, delivered with casual cruelty

jokes.text: Well, at least I didn't get beat up by a girl.
jokes.setup: Ashley complains about having the worst day
jokes.punchline: She points out Will's even worse situation
jokes.explanation: Ashley gets her own dig in at Will's expense

jokes.text: Ow! Argh! Hey. You messed up my count. I hate it when people mess up my count.
jokes.setup: Someone bumps into gym member
jokes.punchline: Overly aggressive response about counting reps
jokes.explanation: Generic gym meathead behavior, not particularly creative

jokes.text: Hey. You messed up my count.
jokes.setup: Helena knocks out another gym member who took her bike
jokes.punchline: Helena's casual complaint about the interruption to her workout
jokes.explanation: Excellent deadpan - Helena treats violence as a minor workout interruption, shows her character perfectly

jokes.text: Okay, but that doesn't leave much for yogurt.
jokes.setup: Stan ordering Carlton to drop and give him 20 (push-ups)
jokes.punchline: Carlton thinks Stan wants him to pay $20 and is concerned about his snack budget
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection - Carlton's complete misunderstanding of military language through his privileged lens is perfect

jokes.text: No, no. When I knocked you senseless.
jokes.setup: Will claiming he was sucker-punched
jokes.punchline: Helena's matter-of-fact correction
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan correction that deflates Will's revisionist history

jokes.text: You know, you have a real aggression disorder don't you?
jokes.setup: Will trying to analyze Helena's fighting behavior
jokes.punchline: Will's pseudo-psychological diagnosis right before getting hit again
jokes.explanation: Good setup for another beatdown, Will's attempt at intellectual superiority before getting humbled again

jokes.text: You know, you have a real aggression disorder don't you?
jokes.setup: Will trying to psychoanalyze Helena's fighting
jokes.punchline: Immediately gets punched for the analysis
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup-payoff. Will's attempt at amateur psychology immediately proves his point

jokes.text: Take me.
jokes.setup: Helena dominating Will physically
jokes.punchline: Will's aroused response to being beaten up
jokes.explanation: Unexpected turn - Will's machismo completely flips to attraction, economical delivery

jokes.text: That was really... impressive. Take me.
jokes.setup: Helena dominates Will in another fight
jokes.punchline: Will is turned on by her dominance
jokes.explanation: Good character reversal - Will's attraction to strong women overrides his bruised ego

jokes.text: Girl, you a little freaky deaky, ain't you?
jokes.setup: Helena's aggressive 'Now' command
jokes.punchline: Will's assessment of Helena's intensity
jokes.explanation: Fits Will's character voice, good reaction to Helena's dominance, mild sexual humor

jokes.text: Code red. Code red. Man your stations, man your stations.
jokes.setup: Tour bus arriving at the fast food restaurant
jokes.punchline: Manager treating tourist customers like military emergency
jokes.explanation: Good workplace absurdism - treating normal business as military operation, manager's intensity is funny

jokes.text: Page 38 of the manual, 'Don't freak out.'
jokes.setup: Ashley having a meltdown during rush
jokes.punchline: Manager references specific manual page about not freaking out
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist humor - corporate manual has specific page about emotional breakdowns

jokes.text: He signed me up for the platinum package. It's twice the price but he promised no rudeness.
jokes.setup: Carlton claiming he told off his trainer
jokes.punchline: Carlton reveals he actually got upsold to more expensive package
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback and character moment - Carlton's 'standing up' to authority results in paying more money, perfectly naive

jokes.text: In the ring or in the jungle?
jokes.setup: Debate about Rocky versus Rambo fighting
jokes.punchline: Will's logical clarification about fighting venue affecting outcome
jokes.explanation: Good logical comedy - venue matters for fictional character matchup, nice button to end episode

jokes.text: In the ring or in the jungle?
jokes.setup: Hypothetical fight between Rocky and Rambo
jokes.punchline: Will asks about venue as if it's a real consideration
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist moment treating fictional character matchup as serious tactical question
